name: Morning at the Museum
venue: Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History
description: Early-access, sensory-safe experience at Smithsonian museums before public opening. Reduced crowds, dimmed lights, quiet spaces, and sensory bags available. Free admission.

name: Jim Scott Community Center Sensory Room Programs
venue: Jim Scott Community Center, 3001 Vaden Drive, Fairfax, VA
description: Full sensory room staffed by a Certified Therapeutic Recreation Specialist offering meditation, adaptive seated yoga, sensory programs for children 3 and under, and teen open sessions. Free with community center membership for Fairfax County residents.

name: AMC Sensory Friendly Films
venue: AMC Theatres (multiple locations)
description: Sensory-friendly movie screenings with lights turned up, sound turned down, and a judgment-free environment where kids can move, talk, and be themselves.

name: Regal Sensory Friendly Screenings
venue: Regal Cinemas (multiple locations)
description: Sensory-friendly movie showings with adjusted lighting and sound levels for a more comfortable cinema experience for individuals with sensory sensitivities.

name: Sensory-Friendly Performances at the Kennedy Center
venue: The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts
description: Modified theater performances with adjusted sound and lighting, relaxed house rules, quiet areas, and pre-visit social stories to help attendees prepare.

name: Sensory-Friendly Shopping Hours at Chuck E. Cheese
venue: Chuck E. Cheese (multiple locations)
description: Sensory-sensitive Sundays and Tuesdays with dimmed lights, reduced sounds, limited menu options, and a calmer environment for children with autism and sensory needs.

name: Autism Swim Program
venue: Various pools
description: Specialized swim lessons designed for individuals with autism, using structured teaching methods, visual supports, and patient 1-on-1 instruction in a sensory-considerate pool environment.

name: Fairfax County Adaptive Recreation Programs
venue: Various Fairfax County facilities
description: Adaptive recreation programs including sports, arts, social clubs, and camp programs for youth and adults with disabilities through Fairfax County Neighborhood and Community Services.

name: Smithsonian Access Programs
venue: Smithsonian Museums
description: Multiple accessibility programs across Smithsonian museums including sign language tours, tactile experiences, verbal description tours, and sensory-friendly events.

name: Special Olympics Virginia Events
venue: Various locations across Virginia
description: Year-round sports training and competitions for individuals with intellectual disabilities including track and field, basketball, swimming, bowling, bocce, and more.

name: Sensory Storytime
venue: Fairfax County Public Library branches
description: Adapted storytime sessions for children with sensory processing differences, autism, and other developmental disabilities. Features visual schedules, fidgets, movement breaks, and a calm environment.

name: Autism Speaks Walk
venue: Various locations nationwide
description: Annual community walk events to raise funds and awareness for autism research and support services. Family-friendly events with activities, resource fairs, and entertainment.

name: Buddy Ball Baseball
venue: Various fields in Northern Virginia
description: Adapted baseball program pairing children with disabilities with volunteer buddies. Players of all ability levels can participate in a supportive, fun team environment.

name: ARTECHOUSE Sensory-Friendly Sessions
venue: ARTECHOUSE, 1238 Maryland Ave SW, Washington, DC
description: Sensory-friendly sessions with lowered sound in the Immersion Gallery, softer lighting, and a designated take-a-break space. Social narratives and experience maps provided in advance.

name: B&O Railroad Museum Sensory Sundays
venue: B&O Railroad Museum, 901 W Pratt St, Baltimore, MD
description: Museum turns off music, provides low lighting, designates quiet areas, and offers sensory-friendly tours, train rides, crafts, and a community resource table. Sensory kits and social stories available.

name: Port Discovery Sensory Friendly Mornings
venue: Port Discovery Children's Museum, 35 Market Place, Baltimore, MD
description: Museum opens two hours early for limited-admission sensory-friendly access with adjusted sound and lighting. Sensory bags with headphones, fidgets, and visual schedules available at front desk.

name: Imagination Stage Sensory-Friendly Performances
venue: Imagination Stage, 4908 Auburn Ave, Bethesda, MD
description: Reduced sound and bright stage lighting, low house lights, limited crowd sizes, glow sticks raised before surprises, designated quiet rooms, and assistive devices available. Pre-visit social stories provided.

name: Adventure Theatre MTC Sensory-Friendly Performances
venue: Adventure Theatre MTC, Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo, MD
description: The longest-running children's theater in the DC metro area offers sensory-friendly and ASL-interpreted showings with a quiet room available inside the facility.

name: Walmart Sensory-Friendly Shopping Hours
venue: Walmart (all US stores)
description: During sensory-friendly hours, stores dim lights where possible, turn off in-store music, and adjust TV wall displays to reduce stimulation for shoppers with sensory sensitivities.

name: Capital One Arena Sensory Room
venue: Capital One Arena, 601 F St NW, Washington, DC
description: The Costabile Family Sensory Room offers a dedicated quiet space during Capitals, Wizards, and concert events. Free sensory bags with noise-canceling headphones and fidget tools available at Guest Services. All staff KultureCity-trained.

name: Audi Field Sensory Room (DC United)
venue: Audi Field, 100 Potomac Ave SW, Washington, DC
description: KultureCity Sensory Room at the top of Heineken Tower features bean bags, interactive light panels, bubble walls, activity panels, and custom tactile artwork by an autistic artist.

name: Nationals Park Sensory Room
venue: Nationals Park, 1500 S Capitol St SE, Washington, DC
description: Quiet Zone Sensory Room with sensory activities, soothing lighting, and soft flooring. Free sensory kits with noise-canceling headphones, fidget tools, verbal cue cards, and weighted lap pads at Guest Services. All staff KultureCity-certified.

name: Robinson Nature Center Sensory-Friendly Sundays
venue: Robinson Nature Center, 6692 Cedar Lane, Columbia, MD
description: Center opens early for self-paced activities including sensory bins, sensory pathways, scavenger hunts, crafts, and planetarium movies with headphones, bean bags, and fidgets. Designed for families with sensory-processing differences.

name: Chessie's Big Backyard Sensory Trail
venue: Lee District Park, 6601 Telegraph Rd, Franconia, VA
description: Accessible 2,160-foot trail with boardwalk through the woods, a sound garden, and multi-sensory features designed for children of all abilities including those with sensory needs.

name: Lorton Community Center Sensory Room
venue: Lorton Community Center, 9520 Richmond Highway, Lorton, VA
description: Small sensory room offering therapeutic recreation programs for individuals with sensory needs. Part of Fairfax County's network of sensory rooms with trained recreation specialists.

name: We Rock the Spectrum Kids Gym
venue: We Rock the Spectrum, Ashburn, VA
frequency: Open play hours daily; structured classes weekly
description: Sensory-safe indoor gym designed for children with autism, open to all abilities. Features zip line, swings, climbing walls, pretend-play area, and fine motor activities. Weekly classes include social skills, yoga, and creative dance.

name: NKH Crusaders Rare Connections Support Group (Morning)
venue: Virtual (Zoom)
frequency: 1st Tuesday of each month at 12:00 PM EST
description: Monthly virtual support group meeting for rare disease caregivers, hosted by NKH Crusaders. Connect with other families navigating rare disease care.

name: NKH Crusaders Rare Connections Support Group (Evening)
venue: Virtual (Zoom)
frequency: 3rd Thursday of each month at 8:00 PM EST
description: Monthly evening virtual support group for rare disease caregivers, hosted by NKH Crusaders. An alternative time for families who can't attend daytime meetings.
